name: Case study: solving P-99 with LPTP and an LLM
summary: arXiv:2607.21196v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Ninety-Nine Prolog Problems (P-99) is a famous set of Prolog exercises. We solved the first thirty three just by prompting an LLM (Large Language M...
description: arXiv:2607.21196v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Ninety-Nine Prolog Problems (P-99) is a famous set of Prolog exercises. We solved the first thirty three just by prompting an LLM (Large Language Model). We used Claude from Anthropic. By solved we mean: generate the Prolog code and a test file, run the tests and check whether they pass, then formally prove types, groundness, termination, unique...
author: Fred Mesnard, Thierry Marianne, \'Etienne Payet, Wim Vanhoof
